sections.title: A: Multiple Choice (Vocabulary)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: People over eighteen can usually ...... in national elections.
Her main ...... is to become a successful architect.
Lack of sleep can ...... your concentration at school.
This jacket is too ...... across the shoulders.
The ...... in this country is usually warm and dry in summer.

sections.title: B: Open Cloze (A prisoner's view)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
sections.passage:
Although I dislike (9) ...... locked in this place, I know I made some terrible choices. If I hadn't broken the law, I wouldn't (10) ...... ended up here. I really (11) ...... I had listened to my family. But nobody can change the past, (12) ...... they?

My room is extremely small – it is hardly bigger (13) ...... a bathroom! It is (14) ...... narrow that two people can't stand side by side. Both (15) ...... us try to keep it tidy, which helps.

My sister lives far away, but she writes (16) ...... me every Sunday. Her letters make prison life easier.

If I leave this year, I (17) ...... never get into trouble again. Freedom will be (18) ...... an amazing gift!

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Emma was five when she first learned to swim. **age**
Emma first learned to swim ...... five.

Why don't you start a school magazine? **set**
You should ...... a school magazine.

How long did it take him to recover from the flu? **over**
How long did it take him to ...... the flu?

Are you helping to prepare the exhibition? **involved**
Are you ...... the exhibition?

We haven't got enough chairs. **short**
We ...... chairs.

He finished the race despite the pain in his leg. **spite**
He finished the race ...... the pain in his leg.

There isn't any sugar left in the café. **run**
They ...... sugar in the café.

It's illegal to park here. **against**
Parking here ...... .

They had to cancel the concert because of the storm. **called**
The concert had to ...... because of the storm.

Thunder doesn't frighten me! **afraid**
I ...... thunder!

sections.title: D: Word Formation (A new painter)
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.
sections.passage:
Were you (29) ...... enough to see the opening night at the Riverside Gallery? I was, and I enjoyed the new (30) ...... of Sofia Martin's paintings. Sofia is not (31) ...... yet, but she is clearly an (32) ...... with a lot of talent.

Her paintings, without (33) ......, are original and (34) ...... . She often paints places of great (35) ...... beauty, like mountains and rivers, but she makes them look bright and (36) ...... . Many of her works are about (37) ...... and hope. It is difficult to become a (38) ...... artist, but Sofia has an excellent chance.

sections.title: G: Error Correction (Wrong form)
sections.instructions: One word in each sentence is in the wrong form. Write the correct form.
